summary: preinstall script fetches remote content and exfiltrates system info to hardcoded external host
details: The package's dist/scripts/preinstall.js runs automatically on npm install and contains three independent high-signal patterns: (1) it requires fs, https, http, and os, collects identity/host information via os.userInfo() and os.networkInterfaces(), and reads process.env (preinstall.js:3-7, 17, 227); (2) it performs outbound https.get() calls to a hardcoded non-publisher host `https://2tak.l.serverhost.name` (preinstall.js:78-80, 192-194); (3) it combines filesystem checks (fs.existsSync) with network I/O in the same lifecycle script. The destination `2tak.l.serverhost.name` is a dynamic-DNS-style subdomain (serverhost.name), not a publisher-controlled CDN, npm registry, or known vendor SDK endpoint, and is unrelated to the package's advertised purpose. This matches the npm-lifecycle-external-fetch attack shape: install-time fetch from a non-publisher, non-registry host with simultaneous system-info collection. A lifecycle script has no legitimate need to contact an arbitrary dyndns host on every installer machine. Installer impact: every `npm install` of this package emits host identity and env-derived data to an attacker-controlled endpoint and potentially executes content returned by it.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
